Money Stuff =0A The nice thing about building an artificial intelligence mo=
del out of a =0Aquantitative hedge fund is that there are interesting ways =
to moneti =0A=0A=0A <> =0A =0A<https://sli.bloomberg.com/click=3Fs=3D825881=
&stpe=3Ddefault&li=3D12441905&m=3D21d6dea82d6b16615cf44233be620525&p=3D0128=
2025>=0A=0A=0A=0ADeepSeek=0A=0AThe nice thing about building an artificial =
intelligence model out of a =0Aquantitative hedge fund is that there are in=
teresting ways to monetize it. A =0Astandalone AI company will probably thi=
nk of ideas like =E2=80=9Csell subscriptions to =0Aan AI chatbot=E2=80=9D o=
r =E2=80=9Csell access to an application programming interface,=E2=80=9D bu=
t =0Awith a hedge fund you can be more creative. A na=C3=AFve approach migh=
t be: =E2=80=9CWe will =0Aask our AI chatbot what stocks to buy, and buy th=
em,=E2=80=9D but that is probably =0Awrong. For one thing, your chatbot is =

=99re barely breaking even,=E2=80=9D Musk =0Areportedlytold employees <> th=
is month =E2=80=94 but one could be optimistic. Probably =0Athe business of=
 selling ads against a right-wing social media site is better =0Athan it wa=
s a month ago; =E2=80=9Cbrand safety=E2=80=9D isless of a concern <> than i=
t was. =0APerhaps there are other cash flows coming. =E2=80=9CAnother miles=
tone for the =0AEverything App: @Visa is our first partner for the @XMoney =
Account, which will =0Adebut later this year,=E2=80=9D X Chief Executive Of=

l thing. Still this all feels a bit speculative; =0Aright now, =E2=80=9Cbar=
ely breaking even=E2=80=9D is not all that exciting for lenders.=0A=0AAnoth=
er possibility: Elon Musk, who owns most of X=E2=80=99s equity, is really r=

=80=9D isn=E2=80=99t quite the word, =0Abut he seems to get a lot of econom=
ic and psychic utility out of it: X is where =0Ahe goes to troll and do pol=

usiness deteriorated, to the point that it couldn=E2=80=99t pay the =0Ainte=

t=0Acover those payments, if the alternative was losing control of his favo=
rite =0Aplatform.=0A=0ABut this analysis seems risky. For one thing, Musk c=
ould lose interest in =0Aowning X. (Hehas before <>!) But also, to believe =
this analysis, you have to =0Athink that =E2=80=9Closing control of his fav=
orite platform=E2=80=9D is a credible alternative. =0A=E2=80=9CIf X doesn=
=E2=80=99t pay its debts,=E2=80=9D you have to think, =E2=80=9Cwe, its lend=
ers, will take it =0Aaway from Musk and own and run it ourselves.=E2=80=9D =
This strikes me as kind of a bold =0Athing to think! If Musk=E2=80=99s lend=
